apply: Students will be able to balance redox reactions using the oxidation number method and half-equation method.
Students will be able to identify and separate the oxidation and reduction half-equations in a given redox reaction.
Students will be able to apply knowledge of redox reactions to predict the products of a reaction and explain the outcome of a reaction.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze and compare the different methods of balancing redox reactions.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of redox reactions in understanding the chemical behavior of elements and compounds.
Students will be able to design and propose a redox reaction to achieve a desired chemical change.
remember: Students will be able to recall the definition and types of redox reactions.
Students will be able to identify the oxidizing and reducing agents in a given chemical reaction.
Students will be able to list the characteristics of redox reactions.
understand: Students will be able to explain the oxidation number concept and its role in redox reactions.
Students will be able to describe the different methods of balancing redox reactions, such as oxidation number method and half-equation method.
Students will be able to explain the importance of redox reactions in various natural and industrial processes.

ncert_objectives: To understand the concept of redox reactions and their importance in chemistry.
To learn the different methods of balancing redox reactions and apply them to various chemical reactions.
To explain the characteristics of redox reactions and their role in various natural and industrial processes.
board_exam_objectives: Students will be able to balance redox reactions using the oxidation number method and half-equation method.
Students will be able to identify and explain the oxidation and reduction processes in a given redox reaction.
Students will be able to apply knowledge of redox reactions to predict the products of a reaction and explain the outcome of a reaction.
